title: The Farmer And His Sons
narrative_elements: dying father tells sons treasure is hidden on farm
sons dig entire farm searching for gold
no gold found but harvest is abundant
sons understand hard work is the treasure

title: The Father, The Son And The Lion
narrative_elements: father dreams son will be killed by lion
father locks son in decorated house for protection
son attacks painted lion in frustration
splinter causes fatal infection
son dies from a lion that was not alive

title: The Fisherman And The Fish
narrative_elements: fisherman casts net
small fish escape through holes
big fish is caught

title: The Monkey And The Fishing Net
narrative_elements: fisherman leaves net on beach
monkey tries to imitate fishing
monkey becomes entangled in net and drowns
fisherman criticizes monkey's lack of judgment

title: The Fishermen And The Stone
narrative_elements: fishermen haul heavy net expecting big catch
net contains only stone and few fish
fishermen grow despondent
old man counsels that joy and grief are sisters

title: The Fishermen And The Tuna Fish
narrative_elements: fishermen catch nothing and prepare to return
tuna fish chased by bigger fish leaps into boat
fishermen rejoice at unexpected catch

title: The Fisherman And The River
narrative_elements: fisherman stirs river to drive fish into net
neighbor complains about dirty water
fisherman says he must disturb river or starve

title: The Flea And The Ox
narrative_elements: flea boasts about biting humans without mercy
flea challenges why ox submits to humans
ox says humans cherish him and rub his forehead
flea laments that rubbing kills fleas

title: The Man And The Flea
narrative_elements: man finally catches flea that has been biting him
flea pleads that it causes little harm
man laughs and declares all evil should be destroyed regardless of size

title: The Flies And The Honey
narrative_elements: honey spills in pantry
flies land on honey
feet get stuck and flies cannot escape
flies lament dying for a brief banquet

title: The Fly And The Mule
narrative_elements: fly harasses mule on wagon pole claiming to control his speed
mule dismisses fly's threats
mule says only the driver's whip truly commands him

title: The Man, The Mare And The Foal
narrative_elements: man rides pregnant mare on road
mare gives birth mid-journey
foal is too weak to travel
foal bargains to be raised in exchange for future riding

title: Hercules And Plutus
narrative_elements: hercules received into heaven for his virtues
gods congratulate hercules
plutus approaches but hercules turns away
hercules tells jupiter he hates wealth for corrupting the world

title: The Horse And The Miller
narrative_elements: old horse sent from battle to mill
horse weeps over lost glory and remembers armor and grooms
miller tells horse to stop lamenting the past
miller says luck changes lives for better or worse

title: The Fox And The Mask
narrative_elements: fox finds theatrical mask
fox turns mask over repeatedly
fox remarks it is beautiful but empty of brains

title: The Tortoise And The Hare
narrative_elements: hare mocks tortoise's slow feet
tortoise challenges hare to a race
fox selected as umpire
hare naps confident in his speed
tortoise wins while hare sleeps

title: The Snake And The Thorn Bush
narrative_elements: grapevine grows next to river
thorn bush used as fence swept away by flood
snake entwined in thorns also swept away
observer comments that the wicked ship suits its sailor

title: The Fox And The Grapes
narrative_elements: fox is hungry and tries to reach grapes on vine
fox leaps repeatedly but cannot reach grapes
fox declares grapes are sour and unripe
fox walks away rationalizing failure

title: The Hare And The Fox
narrative_elements: hare asks fox about her arts
fox invites hare to dinner to show her art
fox has nothing to eat except the hare
hare discovers fox's art is fraud not skill

title: The Fox And The Hare In The Well
narrative_elements: thirsty hare goes down into well to drink
hare cannot escape the well
fox arrives and criticizes hare for not planning an exit first

title: The Fox And The Hyena
narrative_elements: hyenas said to change sex each year
hyena criticizes fox for rejecting her friendship
fox says hyena's changing nature makes it impossible to know if friend or boyfriend

title: The Sow And The Lioness
narrative_elements: sow boasts of large litter of piglets
sow challenges lioness on how many children she bears
lioness replies she bears only one but he is well-bred

title: The Fox And The Lion In A Cage
narrative_elements: fox finds lion imprisoned in cage
fox insults lion rudely
lion says his misfortune not the fox is the cause of his disgrace

title: The Fox, The Lion And The Footprints
narrative_elements: old lion feigns sickness to lure animals into cave
animals enter cave one by one and are eaten
fox greets lion from outside the cave
fox observes only inward tracks and no outward ones and refuses to enter

title: The Fox, The Monkey And His Ancestors
narrative_elements: monkey and fox travel together through cemetery
monkey claims dead people were freedmen of his ancestors
fox notes the dead cannot refute the lie and calls it an opportune moment for falsehood

title: The Fox, The Moon And The River
narrative_elements: fox walks at night beside river
fox sees moonlight in water and mistakes it for cheese
fox drinks river trying to uncover cheese
fox drinks so much she chokes and dies

title: The Fox And The Partridge
narrative_elements: fox flatters partridge on her beauty and suggests she would be lovelier asleep
partridge closes her eyes and fox grabs her
fox opens mouth to say partridge's name and she escapes
both lament their own foolish mistakes

title: The Wolf, The Fox And The Shepherd
narrative_elements: wolf hoards food and rebuffs fox's visit
fox betrays wolf to shepherd out of spite
shepherd kills wolf with lance
fox caught by hunters and killed
fox regrets causing another's death

title: The Foxes And Their Tails
narrative_elements: fox loses tail in trap
fox ashamed of her loss proposes all foxes cut their tails
fox argues tails are unseemly appendages
another fox sees through her self-interested proposal

title: The Fox And The Lion
narrative_elements: fox meets lion for first time and is terrified
second encounter fox is still afraid but less so
third time fox approaches lion and speaks to him

title: The Wolf, The Fox And The Ailing Lion
narrative_elements: lion is sick and all animals visit except fox
wolf accuses fox of disrespect before the lion
fox arrives and claims she searched for a cure worldwide
fox prescribes wolf's warm skin as cure
wolf is killed and fox laughs at her triumph

title: Tiberius Caesar And His Attendant
narrative_elements: caesar walks on his estate at misenum
steward sprinkles hot ground with water to impress caesar
steward runs ahead to repeat the act on next walkway
caesar jokes the effort is futile and the reward would cost more than it's worth

title: The Lion And The Frog
narrative_elements: lion hears loud hollow voice and sees nobody
lion is frightened by unseen noise
lion discovers a frog emerging from lake
lion kills frog in anger after discovering his fear was groundless

title: The Two Frogs By The Road
narrative_elements: frog in deep pond advises roadside frog to move in with him
roadside frog refuses citing attachment to his home
roadside frog is crushed under wagon wheel

title: The Frog And The Mouse
narrative_elements: mouse asks frog to help cross river
frog ties leg to mouse and then dives pulling mouse under
mouse drowns but body floats to surface
kite grabs mouse and carries off frog too
treacherous frog meets same fate

title: The Frogs And The Battle Of The Bulls
narrative_elements: frog watches bulls fighting in the meadow
another frog questions why the frog is worried
frog explains that the loser will flee to the marsh and trample them

title: The Water-Snake, The Viper And The Frogs
narrative_elements: viper and water-snake dispute territory at spring
they agree to fight to determine winner
frogs offer to ally with viper against water-snake
frogs only croak during battle and do nothing
viper wins but is furious at frogs' useless help

title: The Two Frogs At The Well
narrative_elements: frogs' pond dries up and they seek new home
one frog suggests jumping into well immediately
other frog warns that if the well dries up they cannot escape

title: Hermes And The Earth
narrative_elements: zeus fashions humans and orders hermes to bring them to earth
hermes told to show humans how to get food from the ground
earth initially refuses
hermes compels earth by zeus's authority
earth relents but promises humans will toil with groans and tears

title: The Goat And The Vine
narrative_elements: goat eats the vine's shoots indulgently
vine threatens that the goat will be sacrificed
vine declares it will supply the wine for the libation at the goat's sacrifice

title: Jupiter And The Goats
narrative_elements: she-goats petition jupiter for beards
jupiter grants she-goats beards
male goats complain of lost distinction
jupiter says women may have the symbol so long as they cannot match male strength

title: The Swans And The Geese
narrative_elements: swans and geese graze together on meadow
hunters discover them
swans fly away swiftly due to speed
heavy geese cannot take wing and are caught

title: The Goose And The Stork
narrative_elements: stork sees goose diving for food and to avoid hawk
stork offers to be goose's protector against the hawk
goose agrees but when hawk attacks stork does not help
goose is caught and laments trusting a worthless defender

title: The Halcyon And The Sea
narrative_elements: halcyon builds nest on coastal rocks to avoid human hunters
storm causes sea to flood the nest and kill her chicks
halcyon laments that she fled human threats only to be betrayed by the sea she trusted

title: The Hunter And The Horseman
narrative_elements: hunter catches hare and carries it home
horseman pretends to want to buy hare
horseman gallops off with the hare
hunter gives up chasing and sardonically calls it a gift

title: Jupiter And The Hare
narrative_elements: hare envies stag's antlers and complains to jupiter
hare asks for horns for protection and appearance
jupiter warns they will be too heavy but grants the wish
horns weigh down hare and he cannot run and is killed

title: The Roosters And The Hawk
narrative_elements: rooster brings rival before hawk as judge hoping hawk will eat rival
hawk seizes the rooster who brought the case
rooster protests hawk took the wrong bird
hawk declares justice demands he suffer the punishment intended for another

title: The Hawk And The Nightingale
narrative_elements: hawk catches nightingale who begs to be released
nightingale argues he is too small to satisfy hawk's hunger
nightingale suggests hawk pursue larger birds
hawk refuses to release certain prey for uncertain future gain

title: The Widow And Her Hen
narrative_elements: widow's hen lays one egg per day
widow feeds hen more grain hoping for two eggs
hen grows too fat and stops laying entirely

title: The Hen And The Eggs
narrative_elements: hen finds snake eggs and broods over them
swallow sees hen and warns her of grave danger
swallow says the snakes will destroy hen and those around her

title: Hermes And The Statues
narrative_elements: hermes disguises himself and enters sculptor's workshop
hermes asks price of zeus and hera statues
hermes expects his own statue to be most expensive
sculptor says he'll throw in hermes statue for free if others are bought

title: Hermes And The Dose Of Intelligence
narrative_elements: zeus orders hermes to give humans equal doses of intelligence
hermes pours equal portions into each person
short people become wise as they are fully suffused
tall people turn out stupid as the potion never reaches their knees

title: The Man, Hermes And The Axes
narrative_elements: man drops axe in river and weeps
hermes appears and offers golden then silver axe
man identifies only his own axe and is rewarded with all three
greedy friend imitates the scene but falsely claims the golden axe
greedy man receives nothing and loses his own axe

title: The Traveller And Hermes
narrative_elements: traveller vows to give half of anything found to hermes
traveller finds bag of dates and almonds and eats everything
traveller places only pits and shells on altar as promised halves
traveller mocks the god with a literal but hollow fulfillment of vow

title: The Man And The Statue Of Hermes
narrative_elements: man carves hermes statue and tries to sell it at market
man boasts statue brings profit to owner
customer asks why man sells it if it's so profitable
man explains hermes is slow to deliver profits so he needs quick cash now

title: The Man And The Hero
narrative_elements: man makes expensive sacrifices at hero shrine
man constantly spends great sums on offerings
god appears in dream and tells man to stop squandering wealth
god warns that if man becomes poor he will blame the god

title: The Cowhide And The River
narrative_elements: river sees cowhide being swept along in his current
river asks cowhide her name
cowhide says her name is tough
river says he will soften her up

title: The Mountain In Labour
narrative_elements: mountain goes into labour with groaning and rumbling
rumours spread of great expectations across the country
mountain gives birth to a tiny mouse

title: The Oath's Punishment
narrative_elements: man keeps deposited money and plans to swear false oath
man meets lame god Oath at city gate who says he visits every thirty or forty years
man boldly swears false oath the next day
oath immediately returns and drives man to cliff edge
man learns oath returns same day if provoked intentionally

title: The Wolf, The Horse And The Barley
narrative_elements: wolf finds barley in field but does not eat it
wolf leads horse to the barley claiming he saved it for him
wolf says he loves the sound of the horse grinding teeth
horse sees through the wolf's scheme and refuses

title: The Hunter And The Wolf
narrative_elements: wolf attacks flock and tears many sheep apart
hunter tracks down wolf and sets dogs on him
hunter mocks wolf for his fallen strength

title: The Man, His Wife And The Boy Outside
narrative_elements: boy sings at night outside
wife sneaks out to meet him
husband wakes and invites boy inside to end the problem
husband joins in without conflict

title: The Two Hyenas
narrative_elements: hyena has changing nature alternating between male and female
male hyena mistreats female hyena
female hyena warns him to remember how things were
female hyena reminds him she will be male next time

title: The Jackdaw And The String
narrative_elements: man ties jackdaw's foot with string to give as gift
jackdaw escapes briefly when let loose
string tangles in branches and jackdaw cannot fly
jackdaw laments that refusing slavery brought about his own death

title: The Lion And The Unicorn
narrative_elements: lion feigns weakness and borrows unicorn's horn as walking stick
unicorn is left defenceless and lion wounds him
unicorn accuses lion of treachery not cruelty
lion quotes that one should not spare enemies
unicorn replies that victory by guile is dishonourable

title: The Kid, The Wolf And The Flute
narrative_elements: wolf catches kid who is about to be eaten
kid asks wolf to play flute so she can die dancing
wolf plays flute and dogs hear and chase wolf away
wolf regrets being a musician instead of a butcher

title: The She-Goat, The Kid And The Wolf
narrative_elements: mother goat locks kid inside and tells him to open door for no one
wolf overhears instructions and imitates goat's voice
kid looks out window and recognizes wolf
kid refuses entry because despite the voice the wolf looks like a wolf

title: The Seagull And The Kite
narrative_elements: seagull swallows too large a fish and bursts its gullet
seagull lies dying on shore
kite observes and says seagull richly deserves its fate
kite notes that a bird of the air should not seek food from the sea

title: The Sheep And The Injured Wolf
narrative_elements: wolf is mauled and cannot hunt
wolf begs sheep to bring water from the river
wolf promises he'll find food himself if given water
sheep sees through the ruse and refuses knowing it would become the wolf's meal

title: The Rooster And The Thieves
narrative_elements: thieves break in and find only a rooster
rooster begs for life claiming he wakes people for their tasks
thieves say that is exactly why they should kill him since he prevents their robberies

title: The Pigeon And The Painting
narrative_elements: pigeon is thirsty and searches for water
pigeon mistakes a painted water jar on a wall for real water
pigeon flies into wall and dies on impact
pigeon laments that seeking drink caused her death

title: The Lark In The Snare
narrative_elements: lark falls into a snare
lark laments her fate
lark says she brought about her own demise not for gold or silver but for a tiny bit of food

title: A Lark in A Net
narrative_elements: lark is caught in a snare
lark pleads for her life asking what she did wrong
lark claims only to have stolen a grain of corn for hunger
fowler twists her neck without reply and pockets her

title: The Monkeys And The Two Men
narrative_elements: two men taken to land of monkeys
monkey chief asks liar who he is and liar flatters him
liar is showered with gifts for flattery
truth-teller tells monkey chief that he is simply a monkey
monkey chief orders truth-teller attacked for honesty

title: The Lion And The Mouse
narrative_elements: mouse accidentally runs over sleeping lion
lion catches mouse but forgives him as killing small creature brings no glory
lion later falls into pit and roars
mouse hears lion and gnaws through ropes to free him
mouse repays lion's earlier kindness

title: The Shepherd And The Lion
narrative_elements: shepherd loses bull-calf and vows a lamb if he finds the thief
shepherd climbs hill and sees lion eating the calf
shepherd is terrified and prays again
shepherd promises a bull if he escapes the lion alive

title: The Wolf And The Lion
narrative_elements: wolf steals lamb from fold
lion meets wolf and takes the lamb from him
wolf protests the lion took what was rightfully his
lion mocks the notion that a stolen lamb was rightfully the wolf's

title: The Lizard And The Snake
narrative_elements: lizard tries to match the length of a snake
lizard stretches itself to equal the snake's size
lizard bursts apart down the middle from the effort

title: Zeus And Man
narrative_elements: zeus gives animals various gifts of strength and speed and wings
man is left naked and complains he has no gift
zeus says speech and reason are his gift
zeus says it is the greatest gift surpassing strength and speed
man thanks zeus upon understanding

title: The Maids And The Rooster
narrative_elements: widow wakes maids at cockcrow before dawn
maids kill rooster to stop the early wake-up
without the rooster mistress can no longer tell the hour
mistress wakes maids even earlier than before

title: The Merchant And The Ravens
narrative_elements: merchants on journey encounter one-eyed raven
one merchant says the raven is an omen to turn back
skeptical merchant challenges how a bird that couldn't prevent losing its own eye can predict the future

title: The Snail And The Mirror
narrative_elements: snail finds shiny mirror and falls in love with it
snail climbs on mirror and licks it covering it in slime
monkey finds the tarnished mirror and observes the consequences of letting the wrong one walk all over you

title: The Man And His Gold
narrative_elements: miser sells all his property and buries a lump of gold
miser constantly visits the buried gold
workman notices behavior and steals the gold
miser weeps and tears his hair
observer tells miser to replace it with a stone since he never used the gold anyway

title: The Mole And The Frankincense
narrative_elements: mole is blind and claims to see a mulberry
mole claims to smell incense
mole claims to hear a tiny bronze ball
mother says mole has also lost hearing and smell in addition to sight

title: The Mole And His Mother
narrative_elements: mole is blind
mole attempts to kiss his mother but presses against wrong body part
brothers witness the act
brothers remark he has great expectations but lacks even his sense of smell

title: The Monkey And The Nut
narrative_elements: monkey enjoys eating nuts for their tasty meat
monkey tastes bitter shell and throws nut away
monkey discards the nut and loses the nutmeat inside

title: The Monkey And Her Two Children
narrative_elements: monkey gives birth to two babies
monkey smothers favored child with crushing embraces
other child is cast away as superfluous
neglected child goes into the wild and survives

title: The Thief And His Mother
narrative_elements: boy steals teacher's tablet and brings it home
mother receives stolen goods with delight
boy grows into habitual criminal through encouragement
man condemned to death bites off mother's nose as final act
man blames mother for not beating him at the start

title: The Snake, The Weasel And The Mice
narrative_elements: snake and weasel fight each other inside a house
mice are hunted by both predators
mice emerge from holes when predators fight each other
snake and weasel stop fighting and turn together on the mice
